title: Personalization overview: show the right products to the right shoppers

publish.description: What segment rules do, how the rule list and Published/Draft status work, and where to create your first rule.

intro: Personalization lets qSortBy show a different product order to different shoppers — repeat buyers see the categories they love, first-time visitors see your best sellers. You do this with segment rules. This overview covers the rule list, what Published vs Draft means, and how to start a new rule.

steps.heading: Meet the Segment Rules list
steps.body: Open **Segment Rules** from the left menu. Each rule reorders your collections based on **who** is shopping — its own audience conditions and ranking signals. The list shows every rule you've built, with its **status**, how many **shopper groups** it targets, and when it was last updated.

els.text: Open Segment Rules from the left menu — each rule shows shoppers the product order most likely to convert them.

notes.text: These are mocked preview rules — your own rules appear here once the app is installed.

steps.heading: Published vs Draft
steps.body: Every rule carries a **status**. A **Published** rule is live and personalizing your storefront right now. A **Draft** is saved but not yet affecting shoppers — build and refine it safely until you're ready. Use the **All / Published / Draft** filters above the table to focus the list.

els.text: Published is live for shoppers. Draft is saved but not affecting your store yet.

steps.heading: Start a new rule
steps.body: Select **Create segment rule** in the top-right to begin. qSortBy asks how you'd like to start: **describe your shoppers to AI** and let it build the groups, pick a **ready-made preset**, or **build from scratch** by hand. Whichever you choose, you land in the same rule editor and can adjust everything afterward.

els.text: Select Create segment rule, then pick a start method: AI, a preset, or from scratch.

outro: That's the lay of the land: a list of segment rules, each Published (live) or Draft (safe to edit), and a single Create segment rule entry that offers three ways to start. Next, pick the start method that suits you.
